fix: specify linux-x64 runtime for dotnet publish

The 'exec format error' was caused by dotnet publish not explicitly
specifying the target runtime. This caused it to publish for the
host architecture instead of the target container architecture.

Added -r linux-x64 flag to explicitly target x86_64/amd64 Linux runtime.
Also added --self-contained false to create a framework-dependent
deployment that relies on the runtime provided by the aspnet base image.

commit.message:
fix(store): vec0 migration — drop shadow tables before recreating virtual table (#3)

When context_vectors exists as a regular table (old schema), the previous
migration only called DROP TABLE, leaving vec0 shadow tables (_chunks,
_rowids, _metadatachunks*, etc.) intact. A subsequent CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE
IF NOT EXISTS was silently skipped because the shadow tables still existed,
leaving the virtual table in a corrupt / inconsistent state.

Symptoms:
  [BridgeRAG] persist failed (Could not extract metadata value for column message_id at rowid N)
  [BridgeRAG] prepare failed (vectors blob read error for 1)

Fix:
- Detect whether context_vectors is a regular table vs vec0 virtual table
  via sqlite_master (sql column contains USING VEC0 for virtual tables)
- Drop all known vec0 shadow table suffixes before CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE
- Add a corruption-recovery path: if the existing vec0 table throws on a
  simple count(), drop it and all shadow tables then recreate from scratch
- Wrap entire _initVecTables() in try/catch so RAG degrades gracefully

Also syncs the vec0 additions from v1.2.0 dist back into store.ts source:
- upsertVector(): insert/update with sensitive-content filtering
- searchRelevant(): KNN query with time decay + sender weight reranking
- sqlite-vec added as an optional dependency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement IFUNC resolution support (ROADMAP Phase 7.2)

Add support for GNU indirect functions (IFUNC), which allow runtime selection
of optimized function implementations based on CPU features.

Changes:
- loader: Implement R_X86_64_IRELATIVE relocation handling
- loader: Add CallIfuncResolver() to invoke IFUNC resolver functions
- dl: Handle STT_GNU_IFUNC symbols in Sym() and globalResolver
- tests: Add comprehensive IFUNC tests with libifunc.so test library
- docs: Update README to reflect IFUNC support and mark as completed

Implementation details:
- R_X86_64_IRELATIVE relocations call the resolver at relocation time
- STT_GNU_IFUNC symbols are resolved when looked up via Sym()
- Resolver functions are called using purego for proper C ABI compliance
- Both relocation-time and lookup-time resolution paths supported

This enables loading glibc and other libraries that use IFUNC for CPU-optimized
variants of common functions (memcpy, strlen, etc.).

ROADMAP Phase 7.2: HIGH priority feature - COMPLETE
All tests pass. No complexity regressions beyond acceptable thresholds.

Fix FOREIGN KEY crash on anonymous sessions and suppress builtin-tool startup noise (#9)

* Initial plan

* fix: FOREIGN KEY crash on anonymous sessions and suppress Twilio startup warning

- sessions.py: save_context() now does INSERT OR IGNORE to auto-create
  a parent sessions row (named 'anon-<prefix>') before inserting context
  entries, preventing the FOREIGN KEY constraint failure that occurs when
  kernel.run() is called with an anonymous UUID session (one not
  pre-registered via get_or_create).

- kernel/__init__.py: pass None as drivers when loading builtin example
  tools so their setup() is not invoked at startup. Builtin tools are
  reference implementations; setup() should only run for user-installed,
  fully-configured tools. This eliminates the noisy Twilio missing-env-
  vars WARNING on every startup.

- tests/test_sessions.py: add test_save_context_anonymous_session to
  specifically cover the FK-constraint fix path.

commit.message:
feat(tools): add hard timeout safety net for shell processes

Every shell process now has a maximum lifetime ceiling (default 10 min,
configurable via :shell_max_lifetime app env). When exceeded, the process
is terminated automatically and the agent receives a clear error. The soft
checkpoint-based timeout remains the primary mechanism; the hard timeout
is a safety net against runaway commands the agent never stops.

- Schedule hard_timeout timer on process start
- Cancel timer on normal exit or manual termination

commit.message:
feat(CVE-2020-25084): add xHCI usb_packet_copy assertion PoC

- Add CVE-2020-25084/ directory with full exploit and README
- Root cause: xhci_setup_packet() ignores return value of xhci_xfer_create_sgl()
  and usb_packet_map(); when DATA TRB has direction mismatch, SGL is destroyed
  (nsg=0), usb_packet_map returns 0 but p->iov.size=0, then usb_packet_copy
  asserts (0+18<=0) -> SIGABRT
- Key discovery: unmapped PA (0xDEAD…) does NOT trigger because QEMU's
  address_space_map() uses bounce buffers for non-RAM regions (non-NULL return)
- Real trigger: DATA TRB with OUT direction (TRB_TR_DIR=0) for an IN transfer
  causes xhci_xfer_create_sgl direction-mismatch -> xhci_die -> empty SGL
- Update SKILL (reproduce-qemu-cve.md) with xHCI MMIO pattern, bounce buffer
  quirk, NEC xHCI SS_FIRST port layout, and HCSPARAMS1 field encoding
- Rename SKILL file from reproduce.md to reproduce-qemu-cve.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Create rpg-ywes subtasks: unified constraint system with physics integration

10 subtasks for Custom Skeletal Mesh Format with Pose Constraints:

1. rpg-qslo: Unified constraint type system (20 Blender-compatible types)
2. rpg-1blk: Space conversion and constraint evaluation core
3. rpg-s9ob: IK solvers (CCD, FABRIK, Spline IK)
4. rpg-augx: Transform copy and tracking constraints
5. rpg-9sjh: Limit constraints (rotation, location, scale)
6. rpg-ngt5: Transform mapping, action, child-of, pivot
7. rpg-pkgw: Surface/volume constraints (floor, clamp, shrinkwrap)
8. rpg-j8n0: Physics engine integration (ragdoll, bidirectional)
9. rpg-b17x: .fskel format and loader
10. rpg-v00q: Visual test battery (8 tests)

Key design decision: constraints are a UNIFIED system shared by both
the animation pose solver and physics rigid-body engine. Same
constraint_def_t drives both animation bones and physics bodies.

commit.message:
feat(memory): add git-remote scope for tool-call cache

Introduce a new ToolCallCacheLevel type ('system' | 'git-remote' | 'repo')
that replaces MemoryLevel in the tool-call cache system. The new 'git-remote'
level groups all local clones of the same upstream repository into a single
shared cache directory, keyed by a 16-char SHA-256 hash of the normalised
remote URL.

Changes:
- packages/pipeline-core/src/git/remote.ts: new file with getRemoteUrl(),
  normalizeRemoteUrl(), and computeRemoteHash() utilities
- packages/pipeline-core/src/git/index.ts: re-export new git remote helpers
- packages/pipeline-core/src/index.ts: export new types and helpers
- packages/pipeline-core/src/memory/tool-call-cache-types.ts: add
  ToolCallCacheLevel, update ToolCallCacheConfig.level, add level/remoteHash/
  repoHash to ToolCallCacheStoreOptions
- packages/pipeline-core/src/memory/tool-call-cache-store.ts: constructor now
  routes cacheDir by level; add getGitRemoteDir() and getRepoExploreDir()
  helpers; persist dataDir and cacheSubDir as instance fields
- packages/pipeline-core/src/memory/with-tool-call-cache.ts: replace
  MemoryLevel with ToolCallCacheLevel; add remoteHash option; warn + note
  fallback when level=git-remote and remoteHash is absent
- packages/pipeline-core/src/memory/tool-call-capture.ts: add remoteHash
  to ToolCallCaptureOptions for future metadata tagging
- packages/pipeline-core/src/memory/index.ts: export ToolCallCacheLevel
- packages/coc/src/server/queue-executor-bridge.ts: resolve git remote URL
  from workingDirectory at construction time; create FileToolCallCacheStore
  with git-remote level and computed remoteHash when available
- test/git/remote.test.ts: tests for URL normalisation and hash computation
- test/memory/tool-call-cache-store.test.ts: tests for level routing and
  path accessor helpers
- test/memory/with-tool-call-cache.test.ts: tests for git-remote level
  warning behaviour

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
